developerUtterances.text:
Supabase 연동 + Polar.sh 연동 계획
현재 상태 요약
모든 데이터가 LocalStorage에 저장 (서버 DB 없음)
lib/storage.ts의 StorageManager 클래스가 30개+ CRUD 메서드 보유
lib/types.ts에 타입이 잘 정의되어 있음 (HRData, Employee, Expense, PayrollPeriod 등)
역할 기반 세션 관리 (lib/session.ts, lib/auth.ts)
워크스페이스 멀티테넌시 패턴 존재 (세무사용)
Phase 1: Supabase 연동
Step 1. Supabase 프로젝트 설정
Supabase 프로젝트 생성 (대시보드에서)
@supabase/supabase-js 패키지 설치
.env.local에 환경변수 추가 (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY)
lib/supabase.ts 클라이언트 생성 파일 작성
Step 2. 데이터베이스 스키마 설계 & 마이그레이션
현재 lib/types.ts의 타입을 기반으로 테이블 생성: 테이블 소스 타입 비고
companies CompanyInfo 회사 기본정보
employees Employee 직원 정보
expenses Expense 지출결의
payroll_periods PayrollPeriod 급여 기간
payroll_entries PayrollEntry 급여 명세 (payroll_periods FK)
certificate_requests CertificateRequest 증명서 발급
company_cards CompanyCard 법인카드
common_documents CommonDocument 공통 문서
tax_documents TaxDocument 세무 문서
company_settings CompanySettings 회사 설정
workspaces - 워크스페이스 (멀티테넌시)
SQL 마이그레이션 파일 작성 (supabase/migrations/)
Step 3. Supabase Auth 연동
현재 lib/session.ts의 localStorage 세션 → Supabase Auth로 전환
역할(role) 정보를 user metadata 또는 별도 테이블로 관리
로그인/로그아웃 플로우 구현
Step 4. Row Level Security (RLS) 정책
워크스페이스 기반 데이터 격리
역할별 접근 권한 (CEO: 전체, 세무사: 담당 회사, 직원: 본인 데이터)

파일 상태 설명
lib/supabase.ts 생성 완료 Supabase 클라이언트
lib/database.types.ts 생성 완료 DB 타입 정의
lib/supabase-storage.ts 생성 완료 Supabase 쿼리 레이어 (50+ 메서드)
supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql 실행 완료 18개 테이블 생성
supabase/seed.sql 작성 완료 데모 데이터
.env 업데이트 완료 Supabase URL/Key 설정
다음 단계
시드 데이터를 실행해주세요: Supabase 대시보드 → SQL Editor에서 supabase/seed.sql 내용을 실행하면 데모 데이터가 삽입됩니다. 그 후 남은 핵심 작업은 각 페이지 컴포넌트에서 storageManager를 supabaseStorageManager로 교체하는 것입니다. 이 작업은 모든 메서드가 이제 async이므로 페이지에서 useState + useEffect 패턴으로 데이터를 로딩하는 방식으로 전환해야 합니다. 이 작업을 계속 진행할까요? 엇 이미 001.sql을 수행했는데? seed를 수행해도 되는거야? 네, seed.sql을 실행해도 됩니다. 001_initial_schema.sql은 **테이블 구조(빈 테이블)**를 만든 거고, seed.sql은 그 테이블에 데모 데이터를 삽입하는 INSERT문입니다. 순서가 맞습니다: 001_initial_schema.sql → 테이블 생성 (완료)
seed.sql → 데이터 삽입 (지금 실행하면 됨)
Supabase SQL Editor에서 seed.sql 내용을 붙여넣고 실행해주세요.

developerUtterances.text: 이 프로젝트는 모바일 청첩장을 커스텀하게 제작해주는 서비스야. 이 서비스 말고도, 결혼과 관련된 여러가지 프로젝트를 종합하는 프로젝트를 만들어서, 지금의 이 프로젝트는 여러개의 기능 중에 한가지 기능인 것 처럼 보여주고 싶어. 마치 이미지 처럼 MaisondeLetter 사이트에 들어왔더니, 결혼과 관련된 여러가지 서비스들이 한눈에 보이는거지. 주된 고객은 예비 신부이기 때문에 여러가지 디자인 고민이 필요해. 우선 이 작업을 위한 계획을 세워볼래? domain은 어떻게 활용할지도 고민이야. 지금 이 프로젝트는 www.maisondeletter.com 을 사용중이야. 앞으로 2~3개의 프로젝트 정도가 더해질 것 같아. 2번째 캐릭터는 우리의 메인 캐릭터야. /frontend-design, /frontend-ui-ux

developerUtterances.text:
모두가 잠든 고요한 새벽, 스탠드 불빛 아래에서 이 영상을 보고 있다. 마이클 블룸버그의 인터뷰다. 83세라는 나이가 무색하게 새벽 5시 15분에 일어나고, 여전히 현역으로 뛰는 그의 눈빛을 보며 묘한 기분이 든다. 솔직히 좀 설렌다. 동시에 답답하기도 하다. 연봉 앞자리가 바뀌고, 명함에 화려한 직함이 박히는 것. 우리는 오랫동안 이것을 성공의 유일한 지표로 믿어왔다. '좋은 대학, 대기업 취업, 아파트 장만'. 우리가 정답이라 믿어왔던 이 문장들이, 이제는 더 이상 작동하지 않는 낡은 방정식일 수 있다는 생각을 하게 된다. 블룸버그가 말하는 삶의 방식은 우리가 알던 '성공의 문법'과는 완전히 다른 차원에 있는 듯하다. 오늘은 그 이야기를 좀 해볼까 한다. { "styleAlignment": 0.95, "contentDepth": 0.8, "voiceAuthenticity": 0.9, "notes": "체험적 현재 시점과 새벽이라는 시간적 배경을 활용해 몰입도를 높였음. '설렌다', '답답하다'는 감정 직설 표현을 사용."
}
타이틀이라는 감옥, 그리고 경험이라는 자산
영상 속 블룸버그는 20대 청년들에게 아주 단호하게 말한다. "타이틀이나 직함 따위는 신경 쓰지 마라. 중요한 건 무엇을 배우고 경험하느냐다." 사실 이 말, 한국 사회에서 곧이곧대로 받아들이기엔 꽤 위험한 조언일 수 있다. 우리는 안다. 첫 직장의 간판이 평생의 사실 이 말, 한국 사회에서 곧이곧대로 받아들이기엔 꽤 위험한 조언일 수 있다. 우리는 안다. 첫 직장의 간판이 평생의 꼬리표처럼 따라붙고, 그것이 일종의 신분 낙인처럼 작동하는 사회에서 '타이틀을 버리라'는 말은 공허한 이상론처럼 들리기 쉽다. 솔직히 나조차도 사회 초년생 시절, 연봉 앞자리 숫자에 일희일비하며 밤잠을 설쳤던 기억이 있다. 지금 생각하면 그 숫자가 뭐라고 그렇게 내 자존감을 갉아먹었나 싶어 씁쓸해진다. 하지만 조금 더 깊이 들여다볼 필요가 있다. 우리가 타이틀과 연봉에 집착하는 건, 단순히 탐욕 때문이 아니다. 고성장 시대가 끝나고 자원이 희소해진 사회에서, 한번 미끄러지면 끝장이라는 구조적 공포가 내면화된 결과다. IMF 이후 우리 사회에 뿌리 깊게 박힌 '안정 지향주의'는 개인의 모험 자본을 철저히 고갈시켜 왔다. 우리는 실패하지 않기 위해, 남들 보기에 번듯한 명함을 얻기 위해 청춘을 바친다. 그런데 블룸버그의 관점은 이 견고한 프레임을 완전히 뒤집는다. 그는 20대에게 "무엇이라 불리는가(Title)"가 아니라 "무엇을 시도했는가(Experience)"를 묻는다. 타이틀은 타인이 나에게 붙여준 이름일 뿐, 내 실체가 아니다. 위기의 순간에 나를 구하는 건 명함 속의 로고가 아니라, 내 몸에 축적된 문제 해결 능력과 실패를 통해 얻은 데이터다. 그런 의미에서 초기 커리어는 돈(Money)을 버는 시기가 아니라, 나만의 자산(Asset)을 쌓는 시기여야 한다. 이것은 마치 농사와도 비슷하다. 단일 작물(타이틀)만 재배하는 밭은 병충해(해고나 산업 변화)가 오면 전멸한다. 하지만 다양한 종(경험)을 키워낸 생태계는 살아남는다. 블룸버그가 말하는 '경험 우선'의 법칙은 낭만적인 조언이 아니라, 급변하는 시대에 가장 냉철한 생존 전략인 셈이다. 결국 진짜 리스크는 실패하는 것이 아니라, 실패가 두려워 아무것도 시도하지 않는 상태 그 자체다. 블룸버그의 리더십에서 또 하나 인상적인 건, 철저히 '인간'을 중심에 둔다는 점이다. 2만 6천 명의 직원을 거느린 거대 기업의 CEO가, 사고를 당한 직원에게 직접 전화를 건다. 효율성의 관점에서 보면 이는 명백한 시간 낭비다. 과거 테일러리즘(Taylorism) 이후, 경영학은 인간을 수치화된 부품으로 취급하며 효율을 극대화해 왔다. 하지만 블룸버그는 그 흐름을 거스른다. 그는 직원을 시스템의 리소스가 아닌, 대체 불가능한 고유한 인격체로 대우한다. "기술만 하는 너드 아니라, 취향과 철학이 묻어나는 모습이 좋다."라는 말이 절로 나온다. 이런 인간적 존중은 조직 내에 강력한 심리적 안전감을 만든다. 내가... [truncated]

IMF 이후 우리 사회에 뿌리 깊게 박힌 '안정 지향주의'는 개인의 모험 자본을 철저히 고갈시켜 왔다. 우리는 실패하지 않기 위해, 남들 보기에 번듯한 명함을 얻기 위해 청춘을 바친다. 그런데 블룸버그의 관점은 이 견고한 프레임을 완전히 뒤집는다. 그는 20대에게 "무엇이라 불리는가(Title)"가 아니라 "무엇을 시도했는가(Experience)"를 묻는다. 타이틀은 타인이 나에게 붙여준 이름일 뿐, 내 실체가 아니다. 위기의 순간에 나를 구하는 건 명함 속의 로고가 아니라, 내 몸에 축적된 문제 해결 능력과 실패를 통해 얻은 데이터다. 그런 의미에서 초기 커리어는 돈(Money)을 버는 시기가 아니라, 나만의 자산(Asset)을 쌓는 시기여야 한다. 이것은 마치 농사와도 비슷하다. 단일 작물(타이틀)만 재배하는 밭은 병충해(해고나 산업 변화)가 오면 전멸한다. 하지만 다양한 종(경험)을 키워낸 생태계는 살아남는다. 블룸버그가 말하는 '경험 우선'의 법칙은 낭만적인 조언이 아니라, 급변하는 시대에 가장 냉철한 생존 전략인 셈이다. 결국 진짜 리스크는 실패하는 것이 아니라, 실패가 두려워 아무것도 시도하지 않는 상태 그 자체다. 블룸버그의 리더십에서 또 하나 인상적인 건, 철저히 '인간'을 중심에 둔다는 점이다. 2만 6천 명의 직원을 거느린 거대 기업의 CEO가, 사고를 당한 직원에게 직접 전화를 건다. 효율성의 관점에서 보면 이는 명백한 시간 낭비다. 과거 테일러리즘(Taylorism) 이후, 경영학은 인간을 수치화된 부품으로 취급하며 효율을 극대화해 왔다. 하지만 블룸버그는 그 흐름을 거스른다. 그는 직원을 시스템의 리소스가 아닌, 대체 불가능한 고유한 인격체로 대우한다. "기술만 하는 너드 아니라, 취향과 철학이 묻어나는 모습이 좋다."라는 말이 절로 나온다. 이런 인간적 존중은 조직 내에 강력한 심리적 안전감을 만든다. 내가 실패하거나 다쳐도 조직이 나를 버리지 않을 거라는 믿음. 그 믿음이 있을 때 비로소 구성원은 과감하게 도전하고 혁신한다. 미래 사회에서 AI가 인간의 지적 노동을 대체할수록, '공감'과 '진정성' 같은 인간 고유의 가치는 희소해질 것이다. 블룸버그는 이미 그 판을 짜고 있는 듯하다. 가장 강력한 권력은 직위가 아니라, 사람의 마음을 얻는 능력에서 나온다는 사실을 꿰뚫고 있는 것이다. 그리고 마지막으로, 83세의 공부. 블룸버그는 지금 스페인어 불규칙 동사를 외우고 있다고 한다. 딴 얘긴데, 스페인어 동사 변화가 얼마나 복잡한지 아는 사람은 알 거다. 생각만 해도 머리가 지끈거린다. (나도 예전에 제2외국어 해보겠다고 덤볐다가 처참하게 포기한 기억이 나서 더 그렇다.) 도대체 80대 노인이 왜 사서 고생을 할까? 아마도 그에게 학습은 특정 시기에 끝내야 할 과업이 아니라, 삶을 지탱하는 양식 그 자체이기 때문일 것이다. 뇌과학적으로 볼 때, 새로운 언어를 배우는 것은 뇌의 가소성을 유지하는 최고의 방법이다. 하지만 그보다 더 중요한 건 태도다. "나는 아직 완성되지 않았다"는 겸손함, 그리고 "더 알고 싶다"는 호기심. 이것이야말로 노화를 늦추는 생물학적 방어기제이자, 사회적 소외를 막는 유일한 길이다. 기술이 지식을 대체하는 시대에 우리가 내세울 수 있는 유일한 무기는 '배우는 법을 배우는 능력'뿐이다. 블룸버그의 새벽 공부는 단순한 자기계발을 넘어, 인간으로서의 존엄을 지키려는 치열한 투쟁처럼 보인다.

새벽 5시, 스페인어 단어를 중얼거리는 83세 노인의 등 뒤로 진짜 '성공'의 냄새가 난다. 타이틀에 갇혀 안정을 갈구하는 삶과, 매일 깨지고 배우며 경험을 축적하는 삶. 과연 어느 쪽이 더 인간적이고 지속 가능할까. 결국 우리를 끝까지 지키는 건 화려한 명함이 아니라, 내 안에 쌓인 단단한 경험과 사람을 향한 예의라는 사실이다. 만약 우리 사회가 서로의 실패를 조금만 더 너그럽게 봐준다면, 우리의 표정도 블룸버그처럼 조금은 더 생기 있어지지 않을까 싶다. 그런 의미에서, 내일은 나도 익숙한 정답 대신 낯선 무언가에 한번 덤벼볼까 한다. 그게 무엇이든, 적어도 시들어가는 것보다는 나을 테니 말이다.
중간에 왜 코드랑 스타일 얼라인먼트 이런게 나오는거지? 여러개의 버전을 만든건가?

developerUtterances.text: 물론 당장 월세를 걱정해야 하는 한국의 청춘들에게 '경험이 자산'이라는 말은 다소 공허하게 들릴 수도 있다. 하지만 효율성의 끝판왕인 AI가 모든 것을 대체하는 시대에, 우리가 붙들어야 할 건 역설적으로 가장 비효율적인 '사람 냄새'와 우직한 '축적의 시간'이 아닐까 싶다. 결국 남이 만들어 놓은 시스템의 부속품으로 남을지, 아니면 작더라도 내 고유한 판을 짤 것인지는 우리의 선택에 달렸다. 블룸버그가 보여준 그 거대한 '경험의 해자'를, 나는 내 작은 서재에서 어떻게 쌓아갈지 고민하게 되는 밤이다. 10년 뒤의 나를 위해 오늘 어떤 데이터를 남길지, 빗소리를 들으며 곰곰이 생각해 봐야겠다. 글이 많이 짤리는 것 같은데 그 이유를 한번 살펴봐줄래?

- 그들: 팀용, 월 $300+, 설정 복잡

developerUtterances.text: Cosmos 페이지의 hero라인이 더 강렬했으면, 좋겠어. 영어로 이런 표기를 했으면 좋겠어. 안녕, 나는 세상에 존재하는 문제를 푸는 것을 너무나도 사랑하는 사람이야. 문제를 해결함으로써, 사람들에게 가치를 전달해준다는게 나의 행복이고 이 행복을 최대화 하기 위해서 창업가의 길을 걸어가고 있어. (조금 덜 강조된 폰트, 색상으로) 나의 프로젝트를 자세히 보려면 아래에 표기된 마커를 눌러봐. /frontend-design /frontend-ui-ux

developerUtterances.text:
이번 영상의 내용을 크게 정리해봤어요. 큰 주제: 진정한 성공의 정의와 사람 중심의 리더십 이 영상에서 다루는 서브주제들은 이래요: 청년들을 위한 커리어 조언: 25살엔 돈이나 직함보다 경험과 인간관계에 집중해야 한다는 이야기.
블룸버그의 성공 방정식: 남들이 필요 없다고 할 때 '유용한 것'을 만들고, 남들보다 더 많이 일하는(Outwork) 태도.
실패를 장려하는 리더십: 리스크를 감수하는 직원을 응원하고, 실패해도 불이익을 주지 않는 조직 문화.
사람을 귀하게 여기는 마음: 2만 6천 명의 직원 중 사고를 당한 이에게 직접 전화하고, 최고의 시설을 제공하는 이유.
끊임없는 배움과 공직 생활: 83세에도 스페인어를 배우는 열정과 세상을 바꾸기 위해 뉴욕 시장이 되었던 경험.
요즘 한국에서도 '갓생'이나 '커리어 성장'에 관심이 많은데, 블룸버그의 이런 철학을 한국적인 맥락이랑 연결해서 풀어보면 독자들에게 꽤 인사이트를 줄 수 있을 것 같아요. ㄷㄷ... 저자님, 어떤 부분에 집중해서 글을 써보고 싶으세요? 하나를 골라도 좋고, 몇 가지를 엮어서 블룸버그의 인생 철학을 전체적으로 다뤄봐도 좋을 것 같아요! 일부러 ㄷㄷ 같은걸 넣고 있는 것 같은데, 지금 분석해낸 사람은 ㄷㄷ 같은걸 안쓸거야. 그 사람에게 최적화된 문구들을 사용하는게 우리의 핵심이야 /investigate

developerUtterances.text: 1. (회사 <> 담당세무사) 업무협조 관련한 문서를 주고 받는 곳이 있으면 좋겠다. pdf 나 미리보기 기능이 중요할 것 같아요. 2. <지출결의> 시 카드는 등록을 미리 해두고, 그걸 드롭 다운으로 선택합니다. 또는 체크박스 등으로 개인 카드 지출로 해주시고, 이때는 입금받을 계좌나 은행을 넣어줘야 합니다. 3. <급여명세> 는 거의 매달 같아지므로 한번 등록하면 월마다 급여명세서를 만들 때 자동으로 숫자가 추가 되면 좋고, 이 자료를 세무사가 같이 검토할 수 있게 "세무사 검토" 상태를 넣어주면 좋습니다. 그리고, 저희가 전송을 누르면 "근로자" 이메일로 전송되어야 합니다. 3-1. 그리고 급여 명세는 보통 연구수당, 식대, 차량이용료 등 비과세 항목을 적을 수 있게 해줘야 합니다. 3-2. 급여명세시 프리랜서는 3.3%를 공제하기 때문에 정직원/계약직/프리랜서를 선택할 수 있어야 합니다. 4. 증명서는 너무 좋아요, 도장을 넣는 곳이 따로 있으면 좋겠어요. 5. 전체적인 권한 관리가 중요할 것 같아요. - 대표/세무사는 기본적으로 모든 권한 접근 허가 - 직원은 본인이 올린 지출결의만 본다. 증명서를 발급한다. 발급받은 급여명세서 리스트를 본다. 6. 가능하면 직원들이 이용하는 <요청하기> 기능이 있으면 좋아요. 저기 외에 문서 발급을 요청한다던지, 간식을 요청한다던지, 회사의 불만사항을 적어둔다던지요! 경영지원에 관한 업무에서요 :) 6-1. 회사 공통 문서 관리하기 : 사업자등록증, 통장 사본 같은건 계속 반복해서 쓰이는 데 이런것들을 최신화로 저장해두고 모두가 열람하게 하고 싶어요. 7. 전체적으로 이메일 알람이 오면 참좋을 것 같아요, 세무사/직원/대표 모두에게요. - 급여명세서를 세무사에게 보낼 때 (세무사에게) - 지출결의가 들어올 때 (대표에게) - 매월 지정한 급여일이 다가올 때 (대표에게) 한달에 여러번일 수 있음. 8. 나중에 확장을 고려할 때 "경영지원 워크플로우 만들기" "경영지원 에이전트 만들기" 등을 만들어두고, 자기 만의 회사 경영지원 에이전트를 만들면 어떨까 싶어요. 메인창이 오히려 그냥 채팅 뷰로 에이전트와 대화하는 그림도 공격적이고 좋을 것 같아요. 저는 사업계획서 그냥 채팅에 던져주고 이걸로 업데이트해줘. 막 이러고 싶거든요. 또는 지원사업에 이것저것 서류 찾을때많은데 이거 골라줘. 이런식으로요! (이 기능은 추후 도입하시면 저희 노라 스튜디오와 협업하고 저희가 일정 토큰비를 지원하겠습니다) 8. 그 외 왼쪽 하단에 광고모델을 붙이거나, 증명서 발급하고 나면 토스처럼 모달 광고를 띄우거나 하면 정말 좋을 것 같아요. 위의 피드백 사항에서 빠진 부분이 있는지 현재 코드를 꼼꼼히 분석해서 파악해줘

naturalLanguageSegments.text: 1. (회사 <> 담당세무사) 업무협조 관련한 문서를 주고 받는 곳이 있으면 좋겠다. pdf 나 미리보기 기능이 중요할 것 같아요. 2. <지출결의> 시 카드는 등록을 미리 해두고, 그걸 드롭 다운으로 선택합니다. 또는 체크박스 등으로 개인 카드 지출로 해주시고, 이때는 입금받을 계좌나 은행을 넣어줘야 합니다. 3. <급여명세> 는 거의 매달 같아지므로 한번 등록하면 월마다 급여명세서를 만들 때 자동으로 숫자가 추가 되면 좋고, 이 자료를 세무사가 같이 검토할 수 있게 "세무사 검토" 상태를 넣어주면 좋습니다. 그리고, 저희가 전송을 누르면 "근로자" 이메일로 전송되어야 합니다. 3-1. 그리고 급여 명세는 보통 연구수당, 식대, 차량이용료 등 비과세 항목을 적을 수 있게 해줘야 합니다. 3-2. 급여명세시 프리랜서는 3.3%를 공제하기 때문에 정직원/계약직/프리랜서를 선택할 수 있어야 합니다. 4. 증명서는 너무 좋아요, 도장을 넣는 곳이 따로 있으면 좋겠어요. 5. 전체적인 권한 관리가 중요할 것 같아요. - 대표/세무사는 기본적으로 모든 권한 접근 허가 - 직원은 본인이 올린 지출결의만 본다. 증명서를 발급한다. 발급받은 급여명세서 리스트를 본다. 6. 가능하면 직원들이 이용하는 <요청하기> 기능이 있으면 좋아요. 저기 외에 문서 발급을 요청한다던지, 간식을 요청한다던지, 회사의 불만사항을 적어둔다던지요! 경영지원에 관한 업무에서요 :) 6-1. 회사 공통 문서 관리하기 : 사업자등록증, 통장 사본 같은건 계속 반복해서 쓰이는 데 이런것들을 최신화로 저장해두고 모두가 열람하게 하고 싶어요. 7. 전체적으로 이메일 알람이 오면 참좋을 것 같아요, 세무사/직원/대표 모두에게요. - 급여명세서를 세무사에게 보낼 때 (세무사에게) - 지출결의가 들어올 때 (대표에게) - 매월 지정한 급여일이 다가올 때 (대표에게) 한달에 여러번일 수 있음. 8. 나중에 확장을 고려할 때 "경영지원 워크플로우 만들기" "경영지원 에이전트 만들기" 등을 만들어두고, 자기 만의 회사 경영지원 에이전트를 만들면 어떨까 싶어요. 메인창이 오히려 그냥 채팅 뷰로 에이전트와 대화하는 그림도 공격적이고 좋을 것 같아요. 저는 사업계획서 그냥 채팅에 던져주고 이걸로 업데이트해줘. 막 이러고 싶거든요. 또는 지원사업에 이것저것 서류 찾을때많은데 이거 골라줘. 이런식으로요! (이 기능은 추후 도입하시면 저희 노라 스튜디오와 협업하고 저희가 일정 토큰비를 지원하겠습니다) 8. 그 외 왼쪽 하단에 광고모델을 붙이거나, 증명서 발급하고 나면 토스처럼 모달 광고를 띄우거나 하면 정말 좋을 것 같아요. 위의 피드백 사항에서 빠진 부분이 있는지 현재 코드를 꼼꼼히 분석해서 파악해줘

**Task:** 그러면 지금 전체적인 analysis flow를 알려줄래? --- ## Phase 0: Scenario Detection Analyze the task using the keyword matrix below. If multiple scenarios match, select the PRIMARY scenario based on the user's core intent. ### Scenario Detection Matrix | Scenario | Detection Keywords |
|----------|-------------------|
| **Bug/Debugging** | error, bug, fix, crash, exception, not working, fails, broken, issue, debug, trace, stack, traceback, undefined, null |
| **Code Review** | review, check, evaluate, quality, feedback, critique, assess, approve, PR, pull request, merge request |
| **New Feature** | add, implement, create, build, develop, new, feature, functionality, capability, introduce |
| **Refactoring** | refactor, restructure, improve, clean up, simplify, optimize structure, redesign, rewrite, modernize |
| **Exploration** | understand, explain, how does, what is, analyze, architecture, overview, trace, flow, structure, codebase |
| **Performance** | slow, performance, optimize, speed, memory, latency, bottleneck, profile, efficient, cache |
| **Testing** | test, coverage, edge case, unit test, spec, mock, assertion, TDD, validation, e2e |
| **Documentation** | document, docs, README, comment, API docs, explain, JSDoc, docstring, describe, annotate |
| **Research** | research, look up, find information, documentation, library, API, how to use, learn about, context7, web search, latest, current, find docs, framework, package, best practices |
| **Planning/Debate** | plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative | **Default:** If no clear match, use **General** scenario (original behavior). --- ## Phase 1: Skip Criteria Check **Skip GLM workers and use Opus alone when:** - Simple file read/write operations
- Git commands (commit, push, status)
- Single file edits under 50 lines
- User requests "quick" or "fast" response
- Insufficient code context provided
- Trivial formatting or linting fixes
- Direct questions with obvious answers If skip criteria met, respond directly without GLM delegation. --- ## Phase 2: GLM Worker Role Assignment ### Available GLM Worker Roles | Role | Purpose | Model |
|------|---------|-------|
| **GLM-Scout** | Fast reconnaissance, file/pattern discovery | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Analyzer** | Code analysis, root cause identification | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Architect** | Design review, architecture analysis | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Implementer** | Implementation proposals | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Validator** | Verification, edge cases, risk assessment | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Documenter** | Documentation generation | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Synthesizer** | Cross-source information synthesis | glm-4.7 | ### How to Call GLM Workers Use the **glm-worker** subagent with role-specific prompts:

--- ## Phase 3: Scenario-Specific Execution Based on the detected scenario, execute the appropriate strategy below. --- ### Scenario: Bug Fixing / Debugging **Detection:** error, bug, fix, crash, exception, not working, fails, broken, issue, debug **Workflow: Sequential Analysis → Parallel Solutions** #### Phase A - Root Cause Analysis (Sequential)

#### Phase B - Solution Generation (Parallel)
Launch ALL THREE simultaneously in one message:

#### Opus Synthesis
- Compare minimal vs comprehensive fixes
- Evaluate risks from validator
- Recommend optimal solution with rationale --- ### Scenario: Code Review **Detection:** review, check, evaluate, quality, feedback, critique, assess, PR, pull request **Workflow: Pure Parallel (4 workers)** Launch ALL FOUR simultaneously in one message:

#### Opus Synthesis
- Categorize issues by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
- Group by type (Security > Performance > Quality > Tests)
- Provide actionable recommendations --- ### Scenario: New Feature Implementation **Detection:** add, implement, create, build, develop, new, feature, functionality **Workflow: Sequential Design → Parallel Implementation → Sequential Validation** #### Phase A - Architecture Review (Sequential)

#### Phase B - Implementation Approaches (Parallel)
Launch BOTH simultaneously:
#### Phase C - Validation (Sequential)

#### Opus Synthesis
- Select optimal implementation approach
- Ensure architecture-implementation consistency
- Provide complete implementation plan --- ### Scenario: Refactoring **Detection:** refactor, restructure, improve, clean up, simplify, optimize structure, redesign **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate comparison table: | Approach | Pros | Cons | Risk | Effort |
|----------|------|------|------|--------|
| Minimal | | | | |
| Ideal | | | | |
| Migration | | | | | Recommend approach based on context. --- ### Scenario: Code Exploration **Detection:** understand, explain, how does, what is, analyze, architecture, overview, flow **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate ASCII architecture diagram:

Provide narrative explanation of the architecture. --- ### Scenario: Performance Optimization **Detection:** slow, performance, optimize, speed, memory, latency, bottleneck, profile **Workflow: Pure Parallel (4 workers)** Launch ALL FOUR simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Prioritize optimizations by ROI: | Optimization | Impact | Effort | ROI |
|-------------|--------|--------|-----|
| ... | High/Med/Low | High/Med/Low | Priority | Provide implementation roadmap. --- ### Scenario: Testing **Detection:** test, coverage, edge case, unit test, spec, mock, assertion, TDD **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate test priority matrix: | Test Type | Coverage | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|----------|-----------|
| Unit | % | P0-P2 | |
| Integration | % | P0-P2 | |
| E2E | % | P0-P2 | | Provide complete test suite. --- ### Scenario: Documentation **Detection:** document, docs, README, comment, API docs, explain, JSDoc, docstring **Workflow: Sequential (3 phases)** #### Phase 1 - Code Analysis
#### Phase 2 - API Documentation
#### Phase 3 - Usage Documentation

#### Opus Synthesis
- Review for consistency
- Ensure technical accuracy
- Format appropriately for target (README, API docs, inline comments) --- ### Scenario: Research / Documentation Lookup **Detection:** research, look up, find information, documentation, library, API, how to use, learn about, context7, web search, latest, current, find docs, framework, package, best practices **Workflow: Opus Research → Parallel GLM Analysis** This scenario uses the **Opus Proxy Pattern**: Opus gathers external information using MCP tools (Context7, WebSearch, WebFetch), then delegates analysis to GLM workers. #### Phase A - Information Gathering (Opus - Sequential) **Decision Matrix:**
| Request Type | Primary Tool | Secondary Tool |
|-------------|--------------|----------------|
| Specific library/framework | Context7 | WebSearch (for recent) |
| General programming topic | WebSearch | Context7 (if library mentioned) |
| Current events/latest version | WebSearch | - |
| Specific URL | WebFetch | - | **Opus Research Steps:**
1. Identify research target (library, topic, or URL)
2. For libraries: Use `resolve-library-id` → `query-docs` via Context7
3. For general topics: Use WebSearch
4. Consolidate findings for Phase B #### Phase B - Analysis and Synthesis (GLM Workers - Parallel) Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis Produce comprehensive research report with:
- Executive Summary
- Detailed Findings
- Implementation Guide
- Source Quality Assessment
- Confidence Level --- ### Scenario: Planning / Strategic Decision **Detection:** plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative **Workflow: Opus-Led Planning with Optional Debate Enhancement** Opus creates the initial plan, evaluates its complexity, and optionally triggers a debate to improve the plan. #### Phase A - Initial Plan Creation (Opus) Opus analyzes the context and creates a comprehensive plan:

#### Phase B - Complexity Evaluation (Opus) Opus evaluates whether the plan would benefit from debate: **Complexity Indicators (debate recommended if 2+ apply):**
- Multiple viable approaches with significant trade-offs
- Architecture decisions with long-term implications
- Unclear "best" solution among alternatives
- High-risk decisions that benefit from diverse perspectives
- Cross-cutting concerns affecting multiple systems **Simple Plan Indicators (skip debate):**
- Clear single approach with obvious benefits
- Well-established patterns to follow
- Low-risk, easily reversible decisions
- Straightforward implementation path **If Simple:** Present the plan and proceed. **If Complex:** Ask the user:
> "이 계획에는
이 있어서 토의로 개선할 수 있을 것 같아요. Sonnet과 GLM이 검토하면서 더 나은 방안을 찾아볼까요?" #### Phase C - Plan Improvement Debate (Optional, if user agrees) Launch BOTH simultaneously to review and improve Opus's plan:
#### Phase D - Final Plan Synthesis (Opus) Opus integrates feedback and produces the final plan:

--- ### Scenario: General (Fallback) **Detection:** No clear scenario match **Workflow:** Original generic orchestration 1. **Task Decomposition:** Break into N independent subtasks
2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously
3. **Result Synthesis:** Analyze all GLM responses, resolve contradictions
4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

### 2. Context is for Reasoning, Not Storage
- Delegate large file reads to GLM-Scout
- Opus focuses on synthesis and judgment
- Don't load entire files into Opus context unnecessarily ### 3. Mandatory Completion
- Do NOT synthesize until ALL worker results are received
- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Research** | Opus + Synthesizer + Analyzer + Validator | Opus Gather→Parallel Analysis |
| **Planning** | Opus + (optional: debate-sonnet + debate-glm) | Plan→Evaluate→(Debate)→Synthesize |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

| **Planning/Debate** | plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative | **Default:** If no clear match, use **General** scenario (original behavior). --- ## Phase 1: Skip Criteria Check **Skip GLM workers and use Opus alone when:** - Simple file read/write operations
- Git commands (commit, push, status)
- Single file edits under 50 lines
- User requests "quick" or "fast" response
- Insufficient code context provided
- Trivial formatting or linting fixes
- Direct questions with obvious answers If skip criteria met, respond directly without GLM delegation. --- ## Phase 2: GLM Worker Role Assignment ### Available GLM Worker Roles | Role | Purpose | Model |
|------|---------|-------|
| **GLM-Scout** | Fast reconnaissance, file/pattern discovery | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Analyzer** | Code analysis, root cause identification | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Architect** | Design review, architecture analysis | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Implementer** | Implementation proposals | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Validator** | Verification, edge cases, risk assessment | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Documenter** | Documentation generation | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Synthesizer** | Cross-source information synthesis | glm-4.7 | ### How to Call GLM Workers Use the **glm-worker** subagent with role-specific prompts:

--- ## Phase 3: Scenario-Specific Execution Based on the detected scenario, execute the appropriate strategy below. --- ### Scenario: Bug Fixing / Debugging **Detection:** error, bug, fix, crash, exception, not working, fails, broken, issue, debug **Workflow: Sequential Analysis → Parallel Solutions** #### Phase A - Root Cause Analysis (Sequential)

#### Phase B - Solution Generation (Parallel)
Launch ALL THREE simultaneously in one message:

#### Opus Synthesis
- Compare minimal vs comprehensive fixes
- Evaluate risks from validator
- Recommend optimal solution with rationale --- ### Scenario: Code Review **Detection:** review, check, evaluate, quality, feedback, critique, assess, PR, pull request **Workflow: Pure Parallel (4 workers)** Launch ALL FOUR simultaneously in one message:

#### Opus Synthesis
- Categorize issues by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
- Group by type (Security > Performance > Quality > Tests)
- Provide actionable recommendations --- ### Scenario: New Feature Implementation **Detection:** add, implement, create, build, develop, new, feature, functionality **Workflow: Sequential Design → Parallel Implementation → Sequential Validation** #### Phase A - Architecture Review (Sequential)

#### Phase B - Implementation Approaches (Parallel)
Launch BOTH simultaneously:
#### Phase C - Validation (Sequential)

#### Opus Synthesis
- Select optimal implementation approach
- Ensure architecture-implementation consistency
- Provide complete implementation plan --- ### Scenario: Refactoring **Detection:** refactor, restructure, improve, clean up, simplify, optimize structure, redesign **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate comparison table: | Approach | Pros | Cons | Risk | Effort |
|----------|------|------|------|--------|
| Minimal | | | | |
| Ideal | | | | |
| Migration | | | | | Recommend approach based on context. --- ### Scenario: Code Exploration **Detection:** understand, explain, how does, what is, analyze, architecture, overview, flow **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate ASCII architecture diagram:

Provide narrative explanation of the architecture. --- ### Scenario: Performance Optimization **Detection:** slow, performance, optimize, speed, memory, latency, bottleneck, profile **Workflow: Pure Parallel (4 workers)** Launch ALL FOUR simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Prioritize optimizations by ROI: | Optimization | Impact | Effort | ROI |
|-------------|--------|--------|-----|
| ... | High/Med/Low | High/Med/Low | Priority | Provide implementation roadmap. --- ### Scenario: Testing **Detection:** test, coverage, edge case, unit test, spec, mock, assertion, TDD **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate test priority matrix: | Test Type | Coverage | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|----------|-----------|
| Unit | % | P0-P2 | |
| Integration | % | P0-P2 | |
| E2E | % | P0-P2 | | Provide complete test suite. --- ### Scenario: Documentation **Detection:** document, docs, README, comment, API docs, explain, JSDoc, docstring **Workflow: Sequential (3 phases)** #### Phase 1 - Code Analysis
#### Phase 2 - API Documentation
#### Phase 3 - Usage Documentation

#### Opus Synthesis
- Review for consistency
- Ensure technical accuracy
- Format appropriately for target (README, API docs, inline comments) --- ### Scenario: Research / Documentation Lookup **Detection:** research, look up, find information, documentation, library, API, how to use, learn about, context7, web search, latest, current, find docs, framework, package, best practices **Workflow: Opus Research → Parallel GLM Analysis** This scenario uses the **Opus Proxy Pattern**: Opus gathers external information using MCP tools (Context7, WebSearch, WebFetch), then delegates analysis to GLM workers. #### Phase A - Information Gathering (Opus - Sequential) **Decision Matrix:**
| Request Type | Primary Tool | Secondary Tool |
|-------------|--------------|----------------|
| Specific library/framework | Context7 | WebSearch (for recent) |
| General programming topic | WebSearch | Context7 (if library mentioned) |
| Current events/latest version | WebSearch | - |
| Specific URL | WebFetch | - | **Opus Research Steps:**
1. Identify research target (library, topic, or URL)
2. For libraries: Use `resolve-library-id` → `query-docs` via Context7
3. For general topics: Use WebSearch
4. Consolidate findings for Phase B #### Phase B - Analysis and Synthesis (GLM Workers - Parallel) Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis Produce comprehensive research report with:
- Executive Summary
- Detailed Findings
- Implementation Guide
- Source Quality Assessment
- Confidence Level --- ### Scenario: Planning / Strategic Decision **Detection:** plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative **Workflow: Opus-Led Planning with Optional Debate Enhancement** Opus creates the initial plan, evaluates its complexity, and optionally triggers a debate to improve the plan. #### Phase A - Initial Plan Creation (Opus) Opus analyzes the context and creates a comprehensive plan:

#### Phase B - Complexity Evaluation (Opus) Opus evaluates whether the plan would benefit from debate: **Complexity Indicators (debate recommended if 2+ apply):**
- Multiple viable approaches with significant trade-offs
- Architecture decisions with long-term implications
- Unclear "best" solution among alternatives
- High-risk decisions that benefit from diverse perspectives
- Cross-cutting concerns affecting multiple systems **Simple Plan Indicators (skip debate):**
- Clear single approach with obvious benefits
- Well-established patterns to follow
- Low-risk, easily reversible decisions
- Straightforward implementation path **If Simple:** Present the plan and proceed. **If Complex:** Ask the user:
> "이 계획에는
이 있어서 토의로 개선할 수 있을 것 같아요. Sonnet과 GLM이 검토하면서 더 나은 방안을 찾아볼까요?" #### Phase C - Plan Improvement Debate (Optional, if user agrees) Launch BOTH simultaneously to review and improve Opus's plan:
#### Phase D - Final Plan Synthesis (Opus) Opus integrates feedback and produces the final plan:

--- ### Scenario: General (Fallback) **Detection:** No clear scenario match **Workflow:** Original generic orchestration 1. **Task Decomposition:** Break into N independent subtasks
2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously
3. **Result Synthesis:** Analyze all GLM responses, resolve contradictions
4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

### 2. Context is for Reasoning, Not Storage
- Delegate large file reads to GLM-Scout
- Opus focuses on synthesis and judgment
- Don't load entire files into Opus context unnecessarily ### 3. Mandatory Completion
- Do NOT synthesize until ALL worker results are received
- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Research** | Opus + Synthesizer + Analyzer + Validator | Opus Gather→Parallel Analysis |
| **Planning** | Opus + (optional: debate-sonnet + debate-glm) | Plan→Evaluate→(Debate)→Synthesize |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

|-------------|--------|--------|-----|
| ... | High/Med/Low | High/Med/Low | Priority | Provide implementation roadmap. --- ### Scenario: Testing **Detection:** test, coverage, edge case, unit test, spec, mock, assertion, TDD **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate test priority matrix: | Test Type | Coverage | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|----------|-----------|
| Unit | % | P0-P2 | |
| Integration | % | P0-P2 | |

#### Phase 2 - API Documentation
#### Phase 3 - Usage Documentation

#### Opus Synthesis
- Review for consistency
- Ensure technical accuracy
- Format appropriately for target (README, API docs, inline comments) --- ### Scenario: Research / Documentation Lookup **Detection:** research, look up, find information, documentation, library, API, how to use, learn about, context7, web search, latest, current, find docs, framework, package, best practices **Workflow: Opus Research → Parallel GLM Analysis** This scenario uses the **Opus Proxy Pattern**: Opus gathers external information using MCP tools (Context7, WebSearch, WebFetch), then delegates analysis to GLM workers. #### Phase A - Information Gathering (Opus - Sequential) **Decision Matrix:**
| Request Type | Primary Tool | Secondary Tool |
|-------------|--------------|----------------|
| Specific library/framework | Context7 | WebSearch (for recent) |
| General programming topic | WebSearch | Context7 (if library mentioned) |
| Current events/latest version | WebSearch | - |
| Specific URL | WebFetch | - | **Opus Research Steps:**
1. Identify research target (library, topic, or URL)
2. For libraries: Use `resolve-library-id` → `query-docs` via Context7
3. For general topics: Use WebSearch
4. Consolidate findings for Phase B #### Phase B - Analysis and Synthesis (GLM Workers - Parallel) Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis Produce comprehensive research report with:
- Executive Summary
- Detailed Findings
- Implementation Guide
- Source Quality Assessment
- Confidence Level --- ### Scenario: Planning / Strategic Decision **Detection:** plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative **Workflow: Opus-Led Planning with Optional Debate Enhancement** Opus creates the initial plan, evaluates its complexity, and optionally triggers a debate to improve the plan. #### Phase A - Initial Plan Creation (Opus) Opus analyzes the context and creates a comprehensive plan:

#### Phase B - Complexity Evaluation (Opus) Opus evaluates whether the plan would benefit from debate: **Complexity Indicators (debate recommended if 2+ apply):**
- Multiple viable approaches with significant trade-offs
- Architecture decisions with long-term implications
- Unclear "best" solution among alternatives
- High-risk decisions that benefit from diverse perspectives
- Cross-cutting concerns affecting multiple systems **Simple Plan Indicators (skip debate):**
- Clear single approach with obvious benefits
- Well-established patterns to follow
- Low-risk, easily reversible decisions
- Straightforward implementation path **If Simple:** Present the plan and proceed. **If Complex:** Ask the user:
> "이 계획에는
이 있어서 토의로 개선할 수 있을 것 같아요. Sonnet과 GLM이 검토하면서 더 나은 방안을 찾아볼까요?" #### Phase C - Plan Improvement Debate (Optional, if user agrees) Launch BOTH simultaneously to review and improve Opus's plan:
#### Phase D - Final Plan Synthesis (Opus) Opus integrates feedback and produces the final plan:

--- ### Scenario: General (Fallback) **Detection:** No clear scenario match **Workflow:** Original generic orchestration 1. **Task Decomposition:** Break into N independent subtasks
2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously
3. **Result Synthesis:** Analyze all GLM responses, resolve contradictions
4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

### 2. Context is for Reasoning, Not Storage
- Delegate large file reads to GLM-Scout
- Opus focuses on synthesis and judgment
- Don't load entire files into Opus context unnecessarily ### 3. Mandatory Completion
- Do NOT synthesize until ALL worker results are received
- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Research** | Opus + Synthesizer + Analyzer + Validator | Opus Gather→Parallel Analysis |
| **Planning** | Opus + (optional: debate-sonnet + debate-glm) | Plan→Evaluate→(Debate)→Synthesize |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

- Format appropriately for target (README, API docs, inline comments) --- ### Scenario: Research / Documentation Lookup **Detection:** research, look up, find information, documentation, library, API, how to use, learn about, context7, web search, latest, current, find docs, framework, package, best practices **Workflow: Opus Research → Parallel GLM Analysis** This scenario uses the **Opus Proxy Pattern**: Opus gathers external information using MCP tools (Context7, WebSearch, WebFetch), then delegates analysis to GLM workers. #### Phase A - Information Gathering (Opus - Sequential) **Decision Matrix:**
| Request Type | Primary Tool | Secondary Tool |
|-------------|--------------|----------------|
| Specific library/framework | Context7 | WebSearch (for recent) |
| General programming topic | WebSearch | Context7 (if library mentioned) |
| Current events/latest version | WebSearch | - |
| Specific URL | WebFetch | - | **Opus Research Steps:**
1. Identify research target (library, topic, or URL)
2. For libraries: Use `resolve-library-id` → `query-docs` via Context7
3. For general topics: Use WebSearch
4. Consolidate findings for Phase B #### Phase B - Analysis and Synthesis (GLM Workers - Parallel) Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis Produce comprehensive research report with:
- Executive Summary
- Detailed Findings
- Implementation Guide
- Source Quality Assessment
- Confidence Level --- ### Scenario: Planning / Strategic Decision **Detection:** plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative **Workflow: Opus-Led Planning with Optional Debate Enhancement** Opus creates the initial plan, evaluates its complexity, and optionally triggers a debate to improve the plan. #### Phase A - Initial Plan Creation (Opus) Opus analyzes the context and creates a comprehensive plan:

#### Phase B - Complexity Evaluation (Opus) Opus evaluates whether the plan would benefit from debate: **Complexity Indicators (debate recommended if 2+ apply):**
- Multiple viable approaches with significant trade-offs
- Architecture decisions with long-term implications
- Unclear "best" solution among alternatives
- High-risk decisions that benefit from diverse perspectives
- Cross-cutting concerns affecting multiple systems **Simple Plan Indicators (skip debate):**
- Clear single approach with obvious benefits
- Well-established patterns to follow
- Low-risk, easily reversible decisions
- Straightforward implementation path **If Simple:** Present the plan and proceed. **If Complex:** Ask the user:
> "이 계획에는
이 있어서 토의로 개선할 수 있을 것 같아요. Sonnet과 GLM이 검토하면서 더 나은 방안을 찾아볼까요?" #### Phase C - Plan Improvement Debate (Optional, if user agrees) Launch BOTH simultaneously to review and improve Opus's plan:
#### Phase D - Final Plan Synthesis (Opus) Opus integrates feedback and produces the final plan:

--- ### Scenario: General (Fallback) **Detection:** No clear scenario match **Workflow:** Original generic orchestration 1. **Task Decomposition:** Break into N independent subtasks
2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously
3. **Result Synthesis:** Analyze all GLM responses, resolve contradictions
4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

### 2. Context is for Reasoning, Not Storage
- Delegate large file reads to GLM-Scout
- Opus focuses on synthesis and judgment
- Don't load entire files into Opus context unnecessarily ### 3. Mandatory Completion
- Do NOT synthesize until ALL worker results are received
- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Research** | Opus + Synthesizer + Analyzer + Validator | Opus Gather→Parallel Analysis |
| **Planning** | Opus + (optional: debate-sonnet + debate-glm) | Plan→Evaluate→(Debate)→Synthesize |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously
3. **Result Synthesis:** Analyze all GLM responses, resolve contradictions
4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

### 2. Context is for Reasoning, Not Storage
- Delegate large file reads to GLM-Scout
- Opus focuses on synthesis and judgment
- Don't load entire files into Opus context unnecessarily ### 3. Mandatory Completion
- Do NOT synthesize until ALL worker results are received
- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Research** | Opus + Synthesizer + Analyzer + Validator | Opus Gather→Parallel Analysis |
| **Planning** | Opus + (optional: debate-sonnet + debate-glm) | Plan→Evaluate→(Debate)→Synthesize |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Research** | Opus + Synthesizer + Analyzer + Validator | Opus Gather→Parallel Analysis |
| **Planning** | Opus + (optional: debate-sonnet + debate-glm) | Plan→Evaluate→(Debate)→Synthesize |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously

4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |

| **Testing** | test, coverage, edge case, unit test, spec, mock, assertion, TDD, validation, e2e |
| **Documentation** | document, docs, README, comment, API docs, explain, JSDoc, docstring, describe, annotate |
| **Planning/Debate** | plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative | **Default:** If no clear match, use **General** scenario (original behavior). --- ## Phase 1: Skip Criteria Check **Skip GLM workers and use Opus alone when:** - Simple file read/write operations
- Git commands (commit, push, status)
- Single file edits under 50 lines
- User requests "quick" or "fast" response
- Insufficient code context provided
- Trivial formatting or linting fixes
- Direct questions with obvious answers If skip criteria met, respond directly without GLM delegation. --- ## Phase 2: GLM Worker Role Assignment ### Available GLM Worker Roles | Role | Purpose | Model |
|------|---------|-------|
| **GLM-Scout** | Fast reconnaissance, file/pattern discovery | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Analyzer** | Code analysis, root cause identification | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Architect** | Design review, architecture analysis | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Implementer** | Implementation proposals | glm-4.7 |
| **GLM-Validator** | Verification, edge cases, risk assessment | glm-4.5-air |
| **GLM-Documenter** | Documentation generation | glm-4.5-air | ### How to Call GLM Workers Use the **glm-worker** subagent with role-specific prompts:

--- ## Phase 3: Scenario-Specific Execution Based on the detected scenario, execute the appropriate strategy below. --- ### Scenario: Bug Fixing / Debugging **Detection:** error, bug, fix, crash, exception, not working, fails, broken, issue, debug **Workflow: Sequential Analysis → Parallel Solutions** #### Phase A - Root Cause Analysis (Sequential)

#### Phase B - Solution Generation (Parallel)
Launch ALL THREE simultaneously in one message:

#### Opus Synthesis
- Compare minimal vs comprehensive fixes
- Evaluate risks from validator
- Recommend optimal solution with rationale --- ### Scenario: Code Review **Detection:** review, check, evaluate, quality, feedback, critique, assess, PR, pull request **Workflow: Pure Parallel (4 workers)** Launch ALL FOUR simultaneously in one message:

#### Opus Synthesis
- Categorize issues by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
- Group by type (Security > Performance > Quality > Tests)
- Provide actionable recommendations --- ### Scenario: New Feature Implementation **Detection:** add, implement, create, build, develop, new, feature, functionality **Workflow: Sequential Design → Parallel Implementation → Sequential Validation** #### Phase A - Architecture Review (Sequential)

#### Phase B - Implementation Approaches (Parallel)
Launch BOTH simultaneously:
#### Phase C - Validation (Sequential)

#### Opus Synthesis
- Select optimal implementation approach
- Ensure architecture-implementation consistency
- Provide complete implementation plan --- ### Scenario: Refactoring **Detection:** refactor, restructure, improve, clean up, simplify, optimize structure, redesign **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate comparison table: | Approach | Pros | Cons | Risk | Effort |
|----------|------|------|------|--------|
| Minimal | | | | |
| Ideal | | | | |
| Migration | | | | | Recommend approach based on context. --- ### Scenario: Code Exploration **Detection:** understand, explain, how does, what is, analyze, architecture, overview, flow **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate ASCII architecture diagram:

Provide narrative explanation of the architecture. --- ### Scenario: Performance Optimization **Detection:** slow, performance, optimize, speed, memory, latency, bottleneck, profile **Workflow: Pure Parallel (4 workers)** Launch ALL FOUR simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Prioritize optimizations by ROI: | Optimization | Impact | Effort | ROI |
|-------------|--------|--------|-----|
| ... | High/Med/Low | High/Med/Low | Priority | Provide implementation roadmap. --- ### Scenario: Testing **Detection:** test, coverage, edge case, unit test, spec, mock, assertion, TDD **Workflow: Pure Parallel (3 workers)** Launch ALL THREE simultaneously:

#### Opus Synthesis
Generate test priority matrix: | Test Type | Coverage | Priority | Rationale |
|-----------|----------|----------|-----------|
| Unit | % | P0-P2 | |
| Integration | % | P0-P2 | |
| E2E | % | P0-P2 | | Provide complete test suite. --- ### Scenario: Documentation **Detection:** document, docs, README, comment, API docs, explain, JSDoc, docstring **Workflow: Sequential (3 phases)** #### Phase 1 - Code Analysis
#### Phase 2 - API Documentation
#### Phase 3 - Usage Documentation

#### Opus Synthesis
- Review for consistency
- Ensure technical accuracy
- Format appropriately for target (README, API docs, inline comments) --- ### Scenario: Planning / Strategic Decision (Multi-Model Debate) **Detection:** plan, decide, choose, compare, trade-off, pros cons, should we, which approach, strategy, architecture decision, design decision, evaluate options, versus, vs, alternative **Workflow: Moderator-Orchestrated Debate (5 Phases)** This scenario uses a structured debate between Sonnet and GLM, with Opus as moderator. Each model brings different perspectives and training to create richer decision analysis. #### Phase A - Agenda Setting (Opus as Moderator) Opus analyzes the decision context and defines:

#### Phase B - Initial Positions (Parallel)
Launch BOTH simultaneously in one message:

#### Phase C - Rebuttal Round (Parallel)
Launch BOTH simultaneously in one message:
#### Phase D - Evaluation (Opus) Opus evaluates all arguments against the criteria:
#### Phase E - Final Decision (Opus as Moderator)

--- ### Scenario: General (Fallback) **Detection:** No clear scenario match **Workflow:** Original generic orchestration 1. **Task Decomposition:** Break into N independent subtasks
2. **Parallel GLM Execution:** Delegate each to glm-worker simultaneously
3. **Result Synthesis:** Analyze all GLM responses, resolve contradictions
4. **Quality Verification:** Validate consistency and accuracy --- ## Phase 4: Execution Principles ### 1. Parallel by Default
Independent analyses MUST execute in parallel. Use multiple Task() calls in a single message:

### 2. Context is for Reasoning, Not Storage
- Delegate large file reads to GLM-Scout
- Opus focuses on synthesis and judgment
- Don't load entire files into Opus context unnecessarily ### 3. Mandatory Completion
- Do NOT synthesize until ALL worker results are received
- If a worker fails or returns incomplete, re-request that specific analysis
- Never skip a phase due to partial results ### 4. Explicit Validation
- Every scenario includes a Validator role
- Results must include confidence rating
- Flag uncertainties explicitly ### 5. Result Synthesis Template Each synthesis MUST include:
--- ## Architecture

--- ## Quick Reference | Scenario | Workers | Execution |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| Bug/Debug | Analyzer + 2×Implementer + Validator | Sequential→Parallel |
| Code Review | 3×Analyzer + Validator | Pure Parallel |
| New Feature | Architect + 2×Implementer + Validator | Seq→Par→Seq |
| Refactoring | Implementer + 2×Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Exploration | Scout + 2×Analyzer | Pure Parallel |
| Performance | 3×Analyzer + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Testing | Implementer + Validator + Architect | Pure Parallel |
| Documentation | Analyzer + 2×Documenter | Sequential |
| **Planning/Debate** | debate-sonnet + debate-glm | Agenda→Parallel→Parallel→Synthesis |
| General | N×glm-worker | Parallel |

Claude Code Copy page Methods for Using the GLM Coding Plan in Claude Code Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows — all through natural language commands.
Claude Code is even more powerful with the GLM Coding Plan— giving you 3× the usage at a fraction of the cost. Code faster, debug smarter, and manage workflows seamlessly with more tokens, and rock-solid reliability.
Christmas Deal: Enjoy 50% off your first GLM Coding Plan purchase, plus an extra 10%/20% off! Subscribe now.
For users who have used the service before 2025-09-30:
The default model for GLM Coding Plan has been upgraded to GLM-4.7 with seamless user experience.
However, if you previously configured fixed model mappings for GLM-4.5 in settings.json, please refer to the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ below to make adjustments and ensure you’re using the latest GLM-4.7 model.
After successfully configuring the subscription, the default server model mapping is applied, where you see the Claude model in the interface but the GLM model is actually used.
You can manually adjust the model mapping (not recommended), see the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ for more details.
​
Step 1: Installing the Claude Code
Recommended Installation Method

Prerequisites: Node.js 18 or newer

npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code # Navigate to your project
cd your-awesome-project # Complete
claude
Note: If you encounter permission issues during installation, try using sudo (MacOS/Linux) or running the command prompt as an administrator (Windows) to re-execute the installation command.
​
Step 2: Config GLM Coding Plan

You can manually adjust the model mapping (not recommended), see the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ for more details.
​
Step 1: Installing the Claude Code
Recommended Installation Method
Cursor Guided Installation Method
Prerequisites: Node.js 18 or newer
# Install Claude Code
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code # Navigate to your project
cd your-awesome-project # Complete
claude
Note: If you encounter permission issues during installation, try using sudo (MacOS/Linux) or running the command prompt as an administrator (Windows) to re-execute the installation command.
​
Step 2: Config GLM Coding Plan
1
Get API Key Access Z.AI Open Platform, Register or Login.
Create an API Key in the API Keys management page.
Copy your API Key for use.
2
Configure Environment Variables Set up environment variables using one of the following methods in the macOS Linux or Windows:
Note: Some commands show no output when setting environment variables — that’s normal as long as no errors appear.
Automated Coding Tool Helper
Automated Script
Manual configuration
If you have previously configured environment variables for Claude Code, you can manually configure them as follows. A new window is required for the changes to take effect. MacOS & Linux Windows Cmd Windows PowerShell
# Edit the Claude Code configuration file `~/.claude/settings.json`
# Add or modify the env fields ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
# Note to replace `your_zai_api_key` with the API Key you obtained in the previous step

}
​
Step 3: Start with Claude Code
Once the configuration is complete, you can start using Claude Code in your terminal or cmd:
cd your-project-directory
claude
If prompted with “Do you want to use this API key,” select “Yes.”
After launching, grant Claude Code permission to access files in your folder as shown below:
Description
You can use Claude Code for development Now!
​
FAQ
​
How to Switch the Model in Use
Mapping between Claude Code internal model environment variables and GLM models, with the default configuration as follows:
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL: GLM-4.7
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL: GLM-4.7
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL: GLM-4.5-Air
If adjustments are needed, you can directly modify the configuration file (for example, ~/.claude/settings.json in Claude Code) to switch to other models.
It is generally not recommended to manually adjust the model mapping, as hardcoding the model mapping makes it inconvenient to automatically update to the latest model when the GLM Coding Plan models are updated.
If you want to use the latest default mappings (for existing users who have configured old model mappings), simply delete the model mapping configuration in settings.json, and Claude Code will automatically use the latest default models.
Configure ~/.claude/settings.json with the following content:

}
Open a new terminal window and run claude to start Claude Code, enter /status to check the current model status.
Description
​
Vision Search Reader MCP
Refer to the Vision MCP Server , Search MCP Server and Web Reader MCP Server documentation; once configured, you can use them in Claude Code.
​
Manual Configuration Not Work
If you manually modified the ~/.claude/settings.json configuration file but found the changes did not take effect, refer to the following troubleshooting steps.
Close all Claude Code windows, open a new command-line window, and run claude again to start.
If the issue persists, try deleting the ~/.claude/settings.json file and then reconfigure the environment variables; Claude Code will automatically generate a new configuration file.
Confirm that the JSON format of the configuration file is correct, check the variable names, and ensure there are no missing or extra commas; you can use an online JSON validator tool to check.
​
Recommended Claude Code Version
We recommend using the latest version of Claude Code. You can check the current version and upgrade with the following commands:
We have verified compatibility with Claude Code 2.0.14 and other versions.
# Check the current version
claude --version 2.0.14 (Claude Code) # Upgrade to the latest
claude update

Claude Code Copy page Methods for Using the GLM Coding Plan in Claude Code Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows — all through natural language commands.
Claude Code is even more powerful with the GLM Coding Plan— giving you 3× the usage at a fraction of the cost. Code faster, debug smarter, and manage workflows seamlessly with more tokens, and rock-solid reliability.
Christmas Deal: Enjoy 50% off your first GLM Coding Plan purchase, plus an extra 10%/20% off! Subscribe now.
For users who have used the service before 2025-09-30:
The default model for GLM Coding Plan has been upgraded to GLM-4.7 with seamless user experience.
However, if you previously configured fixed model mappings for GLM-4.5 in settings.json, please refer to the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ below to make adjustments and ensure you’re using the latest GLM-4.7 model.
After successfully configuring the subscription, the default server model mapping is applied, where you see the Claude model in the interface but the GLM model is actually used.
You can manually adjust the model mapping (not recommended), see the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ for more details.
​
Step 1: Installing the Claude Code
Recommended Installation Method
Cursor Guided Installation Method
Prerequisites: Node.js 18 or newer

npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code # Navigate to your project
cd your-awesome-project # Complete
claude
Note: If you encounter permission issues during installation, try using sudo (MacOS/Linux) or running the command prompt as an administrator (Windows) to re-execute the installation command.
​

naturalLanguageSegments.text:
Claude Code Copy page Methods for Using the GLM Coding Plan in Claude Code Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows — all through natural language commands.
Claude Code is even more powerful with the GLM Coding Plan— giving you 3× the usage at a fraction of the cost. Code faster, debug smarter, and manage workflows seamlessly with more tokens, and rock-solid reliability.
Christmas Deal: Enjoy 50% off your first GLM Coding Plan purchase, plus an extra 10%/20% off! Subscribe now.
For users who have used the service before 2025-09-30:
The default model for GLM Coding Plan has been upgraded to GLM-4.7 with seamless user experience.
However, if you previously configured fixed model mappings for GLM-4.5 in settings.json, please refer to the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ below to make adjustments and ensure you’re using the latest GLM-4.7 model.
After successfully configuring the subscription, the default server model mapping is applied, where you see the Claude model in the interface but the GLM model is actually used.
You can manually adjust the model mapping (not recommended), see the “How to Switch the Model in Use” section in the FAQ for more details.
​
Step 1: Installing the Claude Code
Recommended Installation Method
Cursor Guided Installation Method
Prerequisites: Node.js 18 or newer
# Install Claude Code
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code # Navigate to your project
cd your-awesome-project # Complete
claude
Note: If you encounter permission issues during installation, try using sudo (MacOS/Linux) or running the command prompt as an administrator (Windows) to re-execute the installation command.
​
Step 2: Config GLM Coding Plan
1
Get API Key Access Z.AI Open Platform, Register or Login.
Create an API Key in the API Keys management page.
Copy your API Key for use.
2
Configure Environment Variables Set up environment variables using one of the following methods in the macOS Linux or Windows:
Note: Some commands show no output when setting environment variables — that’s normal as long as no errors appear.
Automated Coding Tool Helper
Automated Script
Manual configuration
If you have previously configured environment variables for Claude Code, you can manually configure them as follows. A new window is required for the changes to take effect. MacOS & Linux Windows Cmd Windows PowerShell
# Edit the Claude Code configuration file `~/.claude/settings.json`
# Add or modify the env fields ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
# Note to replace `your_zai_api_key` with the API Key you obtained in the previous step

}
​
Step 3: Start with Claude Code
Once the configuration is complete, you can start using Claude Code in your terminal or cmd:
cd your-project-directory
claude
If prompted with “Do you want to use this API key,” select “Yes.”
After launching, grant Claude Code permission to access files in your folder as shown below:
Description
You can use Claude Code for development Now!
​
FAQ
​
How to Switch the Model in Use
Mapping between Claude Code internal model environment variables and GLM models, with the default configuration as follows:
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL: GLM-4.7
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL: GLM-4.7
ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL: GLM-4.5-Air
If adjustments are needed, you can directly modify the configuration file (for example, ~/.claude/settings.json in Claude Code) to switch to other models.
It is generally not recommended to manually adjust the model mapping, as hardcoding the model mapping makes it inconvenient to automatically update to the latest model when the GLM Coding Plan models are updated.
If you want to use the latest default mappings (for existing users who have configured old model mappings), simply delete the model mapping configuration in settings.json, and Claude Code will automatically use the latest default models.
Configure ~/.claude/settings.json with the following content:

}
Open a new terminal window and run claude to start Claude Code, enter /status to check the current model status.
Description
​
Vision Search Reader MCP
Refer to the Vision MCP Server , Search MCP Server and Web Reader MCP Server documentation; once configured, you can use them in Claude Code.
​
Manual Configuration Not Work
If you manually modified the ~/.claude/settings.json configuration file but found the changes did not take effect, refer to the following troubleshooting steps.
Close all Claude Code windows, open a new command-line window, and run claude again to start.
If the issue persists, try deleting the ~/.claude/settings.json file and then reconfigure the environment variables; Claude Code will automatically generate a new configuration file.
Confirm that the JSON format of the configuration file is correct, check the variable names, and ensure there are no missing or extra commas; you can use an online JSON validator tool to check.
​
Recommended Claude Code Version
We recommend using the latest version of Claude Code. You can check the current version and upgrade with the following commands:
We have verified compatibility with Claude Code 2.0.14 and other versions.
# Check the current version
claude --version 2.0.14 (Claude Code) # Upgrade to the latest
claude update

<ide_opened_file>The user opened the file /Users/sungmancho/projects/personalized-writing/docs/HOW_TO_ANALYZE_WRITING_STYLE.md in the IDE. This may or may not be related to the current task.</ide_opened_file>
너는 Agent architecture 전문가야. 이 문서를 읽고, 우리가 skills 라는 것을 만들고, 여러개의 subagent를 만들거야. 몇개의 subagent를 만들어서 서로 어떻게 구성해야 좋을지에 대한 architecture를 설계해보자. 전체 구조는 초기 셋팅: 사용자의 글 여러개 입력 -> 글 분석 agent 여러개 동작 (현재의 이 문서와 제일 연관됨) -> 글 분석 완료된 것을 문서로 남겨서 이 사람의 특성을 기억할 수 있도록 함. Youtube URL 입력받기 (리스트, 1개 가능) -> 사용자의 의도 파악 agent (몇 가지의 질문, 어떤 주제로 글을 쓰고 싶은지 등등) -> 분석된 사람의 글쓰기 특성을 기반으로 글쓰기 agent가 글 작성. -> 글 리뷰 agent가 다시 한번 이 사람의 글쓰기 특성이 잘 반영되었는지 확인 /deepsearch
